title: D1 — Post room: voice-over chain with house clock
brief: Wire a voice-over rig: U 87 through the ISA One preamp into the HD I/O, workstation on DigiLink, monitoring through the loom to the m905 and Genelecs, and slave the interface to the OCX HD master clock.
objective: Record-ready VO chain, monitors fed, one clock master, everything locked.
successMessage: Locked to house clock, +48 V up, VO chain armed. (Full version will route the mic path through the patchbay — bay-1 is on the desk waiting.)
notes: R4 is ACTIVE in the stub: the U 87 (requiresPhantom) must reach the ISA One's mic input with its '+48V' control toggled ON — wiring it with phantom off triggers the R4 teaching toast (see docs/adr/0001, device state). ISA One → HD I/O analog-in runs through the patchbay in the full level; stub keeps the chain minimal.
